frontmatter.title: Resize Elements
frontmatter.description: Resize Elements with the unified Resize API.

questions.question: How do I resize an element?
questions.answer: Use `node.resize()` with options specifying width/height, behavior, and visual detail preferences.

questions.question: How do I resize proportionally by width?
questions.answer: Call `node.resize({ width, behavior: ResizeBehavior.proportional, avoidScalingVisualDetailsIfPossible })` to maintain aspect ratio.

questions.question: What's the difference between contain and cover behavior?
questions.answer: Contain fits the element within bounds; cover ensures the element completely covers the specified area.

questions.question: What does avoidScalingVisualDetailsIfPossible do?
questions.answer: When true, tries to preserve stroke widths, corner radii, and font sizes at their original values.

questions.question: Can I resize with only one dimension?
questions.answer: Yes, use `proportional` behavior (which requires only one dimension) or `contain` behavior with just width or height specified.

questions.question: How do I resize pages?
questions.answer: Set `page.width` and `page.height` properties directly on the PageNode.
